judging.severity_measurement: Impact on the entire Ethereum network
judging.judges: Ethereum Foundation's Protocol Security team, Client Dev Team
judging.poc_requirement_note: All submissions (except informational) must include an executable coded proof of concept at the time of submission

eips: EIP-7594: PeerDAS - Peer Data Availability Sampling
EIP-7823: Set upper bounds for MODEXP
EIP-7918: Blob base fee bounded by execution cost
EIP-7934: RLP Execution Block Size Limit
EIP-7935: Set default gas limit to XX0M
EIP-7951: Precompile for secp256r1 Curve Support
in_scope.scope_restriction: Only Fusaka-specific code is in scope. Any vulnerabilities not specifically related to the upgrade should be submitted via the Ethereum Foundation's Bug Bounty Program.

components: Tests, scripts and other code or documentation that cannot be exploited when the client is running
Anything that cannot be exploited on a live network

reasons.JSON-RPC API: Vulnerabilities requiring the user to have publicly exposed an API, such as JSON-RPC, are explicitly out-of-scope
reasons.Beacon API: Vulnerabilities requiring the user to have publicly exposed an API, such as the Beacon API, are explicitly out-of-scope
reasons.Infrastructure bugs: Such as webpages, DNS, email, etc. are out-of-scope
reasons.ERC-20 contract bugs: Typically not included, however, we can help reach out to affected parties, such as authors or exchanges in such cases
reasons.ENS bugs: Maintained by the ENS foundation, not in scope for Ethereum Bug Bounty
reasons.Tests and scripts: Cannot be exploited when the client is running
reasons.Non-live-network issues: Anything that cannot be exploited on a live network
reasons.Non-Fusaka vulnerabilities: Should be submitted via the Ethereum Foundation's Bug Bounty Program
reasons.High-effort DoS: Sustained, CPU or bandwidth intensive, and/or requires more than 1 packet or on-chain transaction
reasons.Known issues: All TODO/FIXME/BUG/HACK items and publicly known issues (forum posts, PRs, github issues, commits, blog posts, public discord messages, etc.)
reasons.Future upgrades: Items scheduled for future upgrades are not in scope
reasons.Mitigated issues: Issues with existing EIP or public post that would mitigate the attack

specific_exclusions: Vulnerabilities requiring the user to have publicly exposed an API, such as JSON-RPC or the Beacon API
Infrastructure bugs—such as webpages, DNS, email, etc.
Ethereum Naming Service (ENS) bugs (maintained by the ENS foundation)
High-effort (sustained, CPU or bandwidth intensive, and/or requires more than 1 packet or on-chain transaction) single-peer DoS attacks
All TODO/FIXME/BUG/HACK and similar items referenced in any of the codebases
Any publicly known issues (includes forum posts, PRs, github issues, commits, blog posts, public discord messages, etc.)
notes: ERC-20 contract bugs and infrastructure bugs are typically not included, however, we can help reach out to affected parties, such as authors or exchanges in such cases

engine_api.description: EL-CL communication via Engine API is authenticated using JWT and considered semi-trusted
engine_api.rationale: Co-located EL and CL clients communicate via authenticated Engine API (JWT). The Engine API is considered a trusted boundary, but data returned by EL may not be fully verified by CL in all cases (e.g., blob data from engine_getBlobsV2 is used without KZG verification). If the EL is compromised or buggy, corrupted data may be propagated to the network.
security_implications: Engine API is authenticated via JWT
Some data from EL may be used without full verification by CL
Compromised EL can affect CL behavior
engine_api.note: Vulnerabilities in Engine API implementation are in-scope, but attacks requiring a compromised local EL may be considered conditional or lower severity

local_validator.description: Local validator configuration is under operator control and considered semi-trusted
local_validator.rationale: Local validator configuration is under operator control. Misconfigured or manipulated validator settings could cause incorrect node behavior.
local_validator.note: Configuration issues requiring operator error are typically out-of-scope

p2p_peers.description: P2P network peers are unauthenticated and considered untrusted
p2p_peers.rationale: P2P network peers are unauthenticated. Any node can join the network and send arbitrary protocol messages. All P2P input must be fully validated.
p2p_peers.note: Vulnerabilities exploitable via P2P network are high priority for bug bounty

publicly_exposed_api.reason: Vulnerabilities requiring the user to have publicly exposed an API, such as JSON-RPC or the Beacon API, are explicitly out-of-scope

high_effort_dos.reason: Sustained, CPU or bandwidth intensive, and/or requires more than 1 packet or on-chain transaction

known_issues.reason: Any publicly known issues or issues with existing EIP/public post that would mitigate the attack

non_fusaka_vulnerabilities.reason: Should be submitted via the Ethereum Foundation's Bug Bounty Program

future_upgrades.reason: Items scheduled for future upgrades are not in scope

infrastructure_bugs.reason: Such as webpages, DNS, email, etc. are out-of-scope
infrastructure_bugs.assistance_note: We can help reach out to affected parties

erc20_contract_bugs.reason: Typically not included in Ethereum Bug Bounty
erc20_contract_bugs.assistance_note: We can help reach out to affected parties, such as authors or exchanges

ens_bugs.reason: Maintained by the ENS foundation, not in scope for Ethereum Bug Bounty

Critical.criteria: Vulnerabilities that allow an attacker to:
examples: Exploit an EIP/specification or client bug to easily create an infinite amount of ETH which is finalized by the network
Take down the entire network by sending a single malicious on-chain transaction that ends up crashing all clients
Critical.impact: Entire network (>50% validators or all clients)
Critical.attack_vector: Single malicious on-chain transaction or network packet

High.criteria: Vulnerabilities that allow an attacker to:
examples: Trivially cause network splits affecting more than 33% of the network
Bring down more than 33% of the network by sending a single network packet or an on-chain transaction
High.attack_vector: Single network packet or on-chain transaction

Medium.criteria: Vulnerabilities that allow an attacker to:
examples: Trivially cause network splits affecting more than 5% of the network
Bring down more than 5% of the network by sending a single network packet or an on-chain transaction
Medium.impact: More than 5% of the network (or >1% of validators for slashing)
Medium.attack_vector: Single network packet or on-chain transaction

Low.criteria: Vulnerabilities that allow an attacker to:
examples: Trivially cause network splits affecting at least 0.01% of the network
Bring down more than 0.01% of the network by sending a single network packet or an on-chain transaction
Low.attack_vector: Single network packet or on-chain transaction

Informational.criteria: Observations or recommendations regarding code quality, maintainability, or system architecture that do not present a direct security risk
Informational.validity_requirement: Only applicable if the team decides to implement a change based on the report
notes: Informational issues are valid if they don't lead to a sufficient impact to count as Low or higher and are valuable enough for the client that they decided to implement the change in the code
Issues related to parts under the 'Out of scope' section will not be considered valid
Informational issues won't have duplicates, and only the first one is valid
They also don't qualify for the 1st and 2nd week multipliers, as they apply only to issues in the main pot

prohibited_activities: Submitting vulnerabilities not specific to Fusaka
Submitting issues related to future upgrades
Submitting issues that cannot be exploited on a live network
Submitting vulnerabilities requiring publicly exposed APIs (JSON-RPC, Beacon API)
Submitting infrastructure bugs (webpages, DNS, email, etc.)
notes: Severity is measured by the finding's impact on the entire Ethereum network
The Ethereum Foundation's Protocol Security team will assist in judging the findings and have the final word on all judging related decisions
A finding may, if deemed relevant by the judges, have its severity upgraded from the severity definitions
Unrelated findings cannot be chained together and are treated individually
All submissions (except informational) must include an executable coded proof of concept at the time of submission
Only Fusaka-specific code is in scope of this competition

In the case where there is already an EIP that would mitigate an attack, or any type of public post such as a PR, commit, blog posts, public discord messages, etc., the issue is considered a 'known issue' and is not eligible
Vulnerabilities requiring the user to have publicly exposed an API, such as JSON-RPC or the Beacon API, are explicitly out-of-scope
Infrastructure bugs—such as webpages, DNS, email, etc.—are out-of-scope
ERC-20 contract bugs and infrastructure bugs are typically not included, however, we can help reach out to affected parties, such as authors or exchanges in such cases
Ethereum Naming Service (ENS) bugs are maintained by the ENS foundation and are not in scope for Ethereum Bug Bounty
